title: AML with DEK::NUP214 (t(6;9))
clinical_vignette: A 29-year-old patient presents with AML, leukocytosis and peripheral basophilia. Cytogenetic analysis demonstrates t(6;9)(p22;q34).
question: Which additional molecular abnormality is classically associated with AML carrying DEK::NUP214?
explanation: AML with DEK::NUP214 frequently co-occurs with FLT3-ITD and is associated with adverse-risk disease. Recognition of basophilia and the characteristic translocation should prompt integrated molecular assessment.
frcpath_pearl: Peripheral basophilia in AML is an important clue to t(6;9)/DEK::NUP214 and should trigger targeted cytogenetic review.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
